What was wrong / what it is: Side-ad rails (SideAdsFrame) only showed on 5 pages (home/scripts/props/giveaways/marketplace)
Fix / What was built: Added rails to category, script detail, prop detail, giveaway detail, and cart/checkout pages
Why: User asked for rails on every page for max ad exposure

What was wrong / what it is: Booked/coming-soon slots had a heavy box (border+bg); open CTA slots too
Fix / What was built: Removed box from booked/coming-soon; open CTA kept then also removed per final instruction (fully transparent, no forced color)
Why: User wanted a lighter look, then explicitly asked to remove any forced background color entirely

What was wrong / what it is: Rail (aside) had no own background on 5 pages -- root html/body color (#131313) showed through where the page bg only wrapped the inner <main>, not the rail
Fix / What was built: Root-caused it: swapped element nesting so the page's bg-[#0a0a0a] wrapper is OUTERMOST (wraps SideAdsFrame including both rails), matching how home/category already worked
Why: Two earlier band-aid attempts (pinning color on the rail) kept getting reverted/re-broken -- this is the real structural fix
Verified: tsc+lint+build clean, pixel-verified on live site

What was wrong / what it is: Banner image field only accepted a pasted URL, no real file upload
Fix / What was built: Added a real Upload button (POST to /api/upload, purpose=side_banner)
Why: User wanted to upload a file directly, not just paste a URL

What was wrong / what it is: Footer showed Discord + YouTube + X icons, but YouTube/X pointed at the same Discord invite (fake placeholder links)
Fix / What was built: Removed YouTube and X icons, kept only Discord
Why: User asked to only keep Discord since the others were fake placeholders

What was wrong / what it is: Footer showed a generic orange 'F' square + text instead of the real logo
Fix / What was built: Replaced with the actual /fivecrux-logo.png image (same one navbar uses)
Why: User noticed the footer logo didn't match the navbar's real logo

What was wrong / what it is: Featured-row cards got their top edge clipped on hover-lift (translate-y) because the scroll row had zero top padding and a forced overflow-y from overflow-x-auto
Fix / What was built: Added top padding (pt-2, offset with -mt-2 so layout doesn't shift)
Why: Hover lift pushed the card's badge past the container's clipped top edge

What was wrong / what it is: Home page cards were 300px; scripts/props/marketplace/category pages were still 280px -- inconsistent size across the site
Fix / What was built: Changed all listing pages to 300px to match home
Why: User noticed cards changed size depending on which page you browsed

What was wrong / what it is: Grid columns used minmax(300px,1fr) -- with fewer items than a full row the 1fr let columns stretch past 300px, making gaps look huge
Fix / What was built: Capped the grid track at exactly 300px (minmax(min(100%,300px),300px))
Why: User noticed inconsistent gap/spacing on category pages with few results (e.g. Maps: 4 items)

What was wrong / what it is: Scripts whose category doesn't match any active DB category had nowhere to show up in navigation
Fix / What was built: Added an 'Other' entry to the navbar + a manual 'Other' filter chip on the Scripts page that catches any script not in the known active-category list
Why: User asked which pages don't show categories properly / wanted a catch-all

What was wrong / what it is: API already returned the seller's real profile picture (seller_image), but the UI only ever rendered the initial-letter fallback avatar -- the real photo was fetched and thrown away
Fix / What was built: Now renders the actual photo when present, falls back to initial only if none
Why: Found while investigating an unrelated request -- real data existed but wasn't used

What was wrong / what it is: Category badge and framework tags displayed exactly as stored in the DB (often lowercase)
Fix / What was built: Added `capitalize` to both so they always display title-case regardless of raw DB value
Why: User noticed lowercase tags on the product detail page

What was wrong / what it is: Only verified_creator/admin/founder could create coupon codes -- a plain seller with real listings could not
Fix / What was built: Opened access to any seller with >=1 approved script or prop, in addition to the existing roles
Why: User wanted their (or any) seller account to be able to make coupon codes
Verified: tsc+lint+build clean, 3/3 existing coupon tests still pass

What was wrong / what it is: Navbar and side-ad rails each did their own client-side fetch AFTER hydration on every single page load, regardless of whether the page itself was already server-rendered
Fix / What was built: Both route-group layouts ((app) and (public)) now fetch categories + side-banners ONCE server-side and share via React context; Navbar/SideAdsFrame use it immediately, client fetch only as fallback
Why: User reported the whole site feeling slow to load on refresh
Files changed: nav-data-context.tsx (new), lib/nav-data-server.ts (new), app/(app)/layout.tsx, app/(public)/layout.tsx, navbar.tsx, side-banners.tsx
Verified: tsc+lint+build clean -- caught and fixed a self-introduced regression here too (see #15)

What was wrong / what it is: First attempt at #14 used cache:no-store for the side-banners fetch inside the shared layout -- since that layout wraps EVERY page, it silently forced the ENTIRE site from static/ISR to fully dynamic per-request rendering
Fix / What was built: Changed to next:{revalidate:60} matching every other page's own ISR tolerance -- restores static generation
Why: Caught via prod build output showing routes flip from (Static) to (Dynamic) -- fixed before shipping
Verified: Verified via next build output (routes back to Static/ISR)

What was wrong / what it is: category/[slug] was the one listing page still 100% client-fetched (no SSR/ISR), unlike scripts/props/marketplace/home which already were
Fix / What was built: Split into a server page.tsx (fetches scripts+categories server-side, ISR 60s) + category-client.tsx that seeds from that data and skips its own fetch when seeded
Why: Brought the last inconsistent page in line with the rest of the site's SSR pattern

What was wrong / what it is: 'Member since' always showed TODAY's date (new Date()) for every account, regardless of actual signup date
Fix / What was built: auth.ts session callback now also exposes the DB user's real createdAt; profile sidebar reads that instead
Why: Spotted in a screenshot -- obviously wrong output, same date for everyone

What was wrong / what it is: The 'Traffic' section in Profile -> Analytics had no top margin (every other section below it uses mt-4) so it visually crowded/overlapped the 5 stat tiles above it
Fix / What was built: Added the missing mt-4 wrapper
Why: User pointed out visual overlap in a screenshot

What was wrong / what it is: No real per-ad analytics existed anywhere -- impressions/clicks/traffic-sources/geography dashboard didn't exist in the codebase at all
Fix / What was built: Built from scratch: new ad_events table logging every real impression (IntersectionObserver, 50% visible) and click across ads/featured-scripts/side-banners (side-banners had ZERO tracking before this); a 'Detailed Analytics' modal (trend chart, traffic sources from Referer header, geography from Vercel's edge geo header -- no IP ever stored) wired into Profile Ads/Featured Scripts/Side Banners tabs
Why: User asked for a full impressions/clicks/geography dashboard matching a reference screenshot they'd seen elsewhere
Verified: tsc+lint+build clean, 12/12 new tests, 85/85 full regression -- also caught+fixed a timezone bug in the new table before it ever reached prod

What was wrong / what it is: A plain `timestamp` column (no timezone) stores the DB session's LOCAL wall-clock time as a naive value -- reading it back via JS Date shifts it by the session's UTC offset, making date-range queries silently wrong
Fix / What was built: Caught via failing test before shipping -- changed to `timestamp with time zone` on the new ad_events table
Why: Found while writing tests for the new Detailed Analytics feature, fixed before the table ever went to prod (zero cost since it was brand new)

What was wrong / what it is: Two ad-creation upload calls (ads-form.tsx, admin's ad-create dialog) sent bare FormData with no type/purpose field -- silently skipping /api/upload's server-side image-format and dimension validation
Fix / What was built: Both now send type=image, purpose=ad_creative like every other upload path in the app
Why: Found during a site-wide image-field audit requested by the user

What was wrong / what it is: Several image upload fields had no 'recommended size' guidance, risking cropped/stretched uploads
Fix / What was built: Added dimension hints: ad creative 1200x800px, side-banner 300x600px, script/giveaway cover 1280x720 (now matches submit pages), avatar square min 200x200px (tooltip)
Why: Full site audit requested by the user to make sure every image field has upload + sizing guidance
Files changed: ads-form.tsx, admin/page.tsx, side-banners-manager.tsx, profile/scripts/[id]/edit, profile/giveaways/[id]/edit, profile/page.tsx

What was wrong / what it is: No storewide affiliate/referral code system existed -- only self-scoped Coupons (discount your own listing)
Fix / What was built: Built a new, separate 'Creator Codes' feature: any buyer on any cart can use a creator's code to get a discount, and that creator earns a tracked commission on the sale. New Coupon/Creator dropdown toggle at checkout (mirrors Tebex's own UI); Profile -> Creator Codes tab to create/manage + see real earnings (summed from actual redemptions, no drifting counter). Commission tracking only -- payouts are manual, same as every other money feature in this app
Why: User saw Tebex's own Coupon/Creator toggle on a script purchase and wanted the same for FiveCrux's own cart (props/side-banner slots)
Files changed: lib/creator-code-access.ts, lib/creator-code-utils.ts (new), creator_codes + creator_code_redemptions tables + migration, /api/creator-codes, /api/cart/creator-code, tebex-checkout-flow.ts, cart-checkout-panel.tsx, creator-codes-tab.tsx (new)
Verified: tsc+lint+build clean, 9/9 new tests, 94/94 full regression

What was wrong / what it is: The 'Featured' row below the hero showed the SAME first 5 items as the hero spotlight carousel just above it -- literal duplicate content
Fix / What was built: Featured row now skips the first 5 (shows items 6-15 of the featured/fallback list) so it never repeats what the hero already showed
Why: User pointed out the hero's big featured item was now duplicated in the row below since the hero got bigger

What was wrong / what it is: Asked to confirm New Releases shows newest-first and auto-updates
Fix / What was built: Already correct -- /api/scripts orders by createdAt DESC and the row just takes the first 12, so it's always live DB data, always newest-first, no hardcoding
Why: No fix needed -- confirmed via the actual DB query, not assumption

What was wrong / what it is: Row was labeled 'Free Scripts' even though free items can include props/maps/etc, not just scripts
Fix / What was built: Renamed the row title to 'Free Assets'
Why: User asked to rename it since the category includes more than just scripts

What was wrong / what it is: A promotional strip ('Got scripts to sell? ... Start Selling') sat between the discovery rows and the 'Why Choose FiveCrux' section
Fix / What was built: Removed the entire section
Why: User asked to remove this banner

What was wrong / what it is: Repeatedly pushed commits to the feature branch after its PR had already merged -- those later commits sat unmerged with no PR tracking them, so fixes felt 'stuck' even though nothing was actually broken
Fix / What was built: Diagnosed via comparing git log on main vs the feature branch + checking the actual deployed JS bundle content (not just PR/CI status labels, which can be misleading) -- created catch-up PRs each time, then switched to pushing directly to main per explicit user instruction
Why: This was the root cause of several 'why isn't my fix live' confusions this session -- documented so it doesn't recur
Verified: Confirmed by content-diffing the live JS bundle before/after each fix
